title: Sport
overview: Sport is an important part of life in the United Kingdom. It promotes health, teamwork, and national pride. Many of the world's most popular sports—including football, rugby, cricket, golf, and tennis—were either invented or developed in Britain. Sport brings communities together and celebrates fair play, effort, and respect.

study_sections.heading: Football
study_sections.content: Most popular UK sport. Governed by Football Association (FA), founded 1863. FA Cup first played 1871—world's oldest football competition. Famous teams: Manchester United, Liverpool, Arsenal, Celtic, Rangers.

study_sections.heading: Rugby and Cricket
study_sections.content: Rugby originated at Rugby School, England. Two types: Rugby Union and Rugby League. Cricket originated in England, played in Commonwealth countries. The Ashes: England vs Australia cricket series.

study_sections.heading: Tennis and Golf
study_sections.content: Wimbledon Championships began 1877—oldest tennis tournament. St Andrews, Scotland is 'home of golf.' Fair play represents British sporting values.

study_sections.heading: Olympics and Athletes
study_sections.content: London hosted Olympics three times: 1908, 1948, 2012. Mo Farah won two gold medals in 2012 for running. Andy Murray won Wimbledon 2013. Lewis Hamilton: multiple F1 world titles. Bradley Wiggins: first British Tour de France winner (2012).

key_facts: Two rugby types: Union and League
Cricket: England vs Australia = The Ashes
Home of golf: St Andrews, Scotland
memory_hook: UK sports as invention timeline: 1800s (football FA 1863, rugby at Rugby School, cricket centuries old, golf at St Andrews, tennis Wimbledon 1877, FA Cup 1871), 1900s (Olympics in London 1908/1948/2012), 2000s (Murray Wimbledon 2013, Wiggins Tour 2012, Farah Olympics 2012, Hamilton F1). Fair play thread runs through all—teamwork, respect, community!

questions.prompt: When was FA Cup first played?

supportive_messages.high_score: Excellent! You know UK sport brilliantly! ⚽
supportive_messages.medium_score: Good work! Review sports and athletes! 📚
